content:
**Donald Trump Wins 2024 Popular Vote in Historic Presidential Race**

November 6, 2024

In a stunning turn of events, former President Donald Trump has won the popular vote in the 2024 presidential election, according to final tallies released this morning. This marks the first time Trump has secured the nationwide popular vote, surpassing President Joe Biden and a crowded field of third-party and independent candidates.

With more than 156 million votes cast, Trump garnered an estimated 49.1% of the popular vote, besting Biden’s 48.2%, according to certified results from the Federal Election Commission. Robert F. Kennedy Jr., running as an independent, drew a noteworthy 2.3%, siphoning votes from both major parties.

“I am deeply honored by the trust the American people have placed in me,” Trump said to supporters in Palm Beach, Florida, early Wednesday. “This is not just a victory for me, but for every American who wants to see our country strong, safe, and prosperous again.”

The outcome surprised many analysts, as polls throughout the summer and fall showed a razor-thin race favoring Biden. “Trump’s ability to mobilize his base and appeal to working-class voters in key states made the difference,” said Dr. Emily Carter, a political scientist at Georgetown University.

This result is historic: it is the first time since George W. Bush in 2004 that a Republican candidate has won the popular vote in a presidential election. The race now turns to the Electoral College, whose final certification is expected in December.
